description_long: This object is a solid-body electric guitar with a distinctive V-shaped body, reminiscent of the classic 'Flying V' design. The body is primarily white with bold black geometric accents, creating a striking visual contrast. The neck is long and slender, featuring a series of rectangular inlays along the fretboard, and the headstock is angular, matching the sharp lines of the body. The bridge, pickups, and control knobs are visible on the body, all rendered in black, further emphasizing the instrument's modern, aggressive aesthetic. The guitar appears to be constructed from a combination of wood and metal, with a glossy finish that reflects light. This instrument would be well-suited for urban environments such as music venues, street performances, or garage bands.
description: A white and black V-shaped electric guitar with a long neck, geometric accents, and a glossy finish. The design is bold and modern, featuring visible pickups and control hardware.
description_view_0: The guitar's V-shaped body and long neck are clearly visible, with the black geometric accents and hardware standing out against the white body.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the thickness of the body and the sharp, angular contours of the V shape, as well as the placement of the pickups and bridge.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the V body is visible, showing the symmetry of the design and the continuation of the black accents along the edge.
description_view_3: The view from the rear of the guitar emphasizes the back of the V body and the length of the neck, with less detail visible on the hardware.
attribute_headstock_design: Angular headstock with inline tuning pegs, no visible logo
